purpose: Premium bakhoor burner (mabkhara) with oud wood chips and Arabic home fragrance products in authentic Saudi home ambiance. Core Saudi lifestyle content.

tone_descriptor.description: Tone direction: contemplative, bold, warm, dignified, kinetic, etc.

prompt_template: Premium bakhoor and oud home fragrance product photography, {mabkhara_descriptor} traditional Arabic incense burner with glowing embers and thin smoke curl rising, oud wood chips visible in ornate brass or ceramic bowl, {featured_fragrance_product} as hero item, {supporting_products} — oud oil bottles, room spray, reed diffuser, arranged on {surface_descriptor} — carved wooden tray, marble, dark polished stone, warm amber and deep gold ambient lighting suggesting Saudi majlis interior atmosphere, richly textured Arabic cushions or textile visible in soft background bokeh, hyper-realistic smoke and metalwork detail, shot on 50mm at f/4, no text, no watermark {cultural_constraints_injection}
anti_patterns: Avoid in output: Western candle aesthetic (must feel Arabic)
Avoid in output: alcohol-based fragrance signals
notes: Intents: grow, harvest. Saudi adaptation guidance: Bakhoor (oud-chip incense) burning is a daily ritual in Saudi and Gulf homes — among the most culturally resonant content categories. Mabkhara (incense burner) is an iconic Saudi object. Oud and bakhoor brands have some of the highest engagement rates in Saudi lifestyle content. Reference image: Optional. Recommended frequency: 2-3 per week for fragrance and home brands. Compliance level: standard.
